is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study examines Plasmodium falciparum infection prevalence in western Kenya residents.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Paper studies human sickle haemoglobin (HbS) genotypes and malaria parasite prevalence; no P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfk13, etc.) are examined.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Reports malaria prevalence by host genotype, not parasite drug-resistance codon frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: Kisumu area / Western Kenya near Lake Victoria - sub-national location given.

collection_window_within_3y.evidence: 'Blood samples were taken from 773 subjects over a period of 12 months.'

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical samples from West Kenyan residents of the Kisumu area.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Single-site cross-sectional survey, no mention of consortium or shared dataset.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary materials referenced; the study does not concern drug-resistance markers regardless.

assessment.summary: This 1997 cross-sectional study examines the protective effect of sickle cell haemoglobin genotypes (HbAA/HbAS/HbSS) against P. falciparum malaria infection in West Kenyan residents of the Kisumu area. It reports malaria prevalence by host genotype but contains no parasite drug-resistance marker data.
exclusion_reasons: No curated P. falciparum drug-resistance markers reported
No extractable per-locus drug-resistance frequencies; study is about host sickle cell genetics and malaria prevalence
